body: PT Techno9 Indonesia Tbk (TECH) will conduct a rights issue worth IDR 3.3 trillion, conditional on a share purchase agreement with Nine Poh Group, which will acquire 70% of Nine's shares. The agreement requires Nine to hold two rights issues, worth IDR 80 billion and USD 200 million, and acquire a majority stake in Poh Resources Pte. Ltd., with a mandatory tender offer to follow if the takeover plan is completed.
title: PT Techno9 Indonesia to raise IDR 3.3 trillion through rights issue as part of conditional share purchase agreement with Singapore's Poh Group.

body: TRIN signed a credit agreement worth IDR 25 billion on January 24, 2025, with a 23-month tenor and a 10% annual interest rate to support operational construction costs for its ongoing Sequoia Hills project in Sentul, Bogor. The credit facility will be used for working capital, according to President Director Ishak Chandra.
title: TRIN Secures IDR 25 Billion Credit Facility to Support Operational Construction Costs.

body: The Indonesian Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources will evaluate the work plans and budgets of nickel mining companies for the 2024-2026 period to prevent reckless production and ensure post-mining reclamation guarantees, which may lead to production cuts. Indonesia's nickel ore production is expected to reach 215 million tonnes per year in 2024, up from 175.6 million tonnes in 2023, and the ministry aims to maintain selling prices in the global market, where prices recently hit a four-year low of $14,767.50 per tonne.
title: Indonesia to Evaluate Nickel Mining Companies' Work Plans to Ensure Sustainable Production.

body: PT Mitrajasa Sentosa Cemerlang, a subsidiary of PT Mitrabara Adiperdana TBK (MBAP), has secured a IDR 3 billion loan facility from its parent company, BDMS, with an 8% annual interest rate, which will be used to support the company's working capital. The loan, categorized as an affiliated transaction, has a 12-month tenure with the option to extend up to two times, and interest will only be charged if PT Mitrabara Adiperdana TBK (MBAP) achieves operating profit.
title: Mitrajasa Sentosa Cemerlang secures IDR 3 billion loan from parent company BDMS to support working capital.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) closed down 0.92% or 66.59 points to 7,166.06 on Friday, with PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) being the top laggards, correcting 16.90 and 16.27 index points, respectively. Meanwhile, PT Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua Tbk led the top leaders list, contributing 6.96 index points, and foreign investors recorded a net sell of IDR 571.49 billion, targeting stocks such as BBRI, BBCA, and PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO).
title: Jakarta Composite Index Closes Down 0.92% Amid Foreign Net Selling

body: PT Bank UOB Indonesia (UOB) expects Indonesia's inflation rate to rise to 2.5% by the end of 2025, up from 2.3% in December 2024, driven by higher food prices due to the El Nino phenomenon and a weakening rupiah against the US dollar. The bank's economists predict that the rupiah will break IDR16,500 by the end of 2025, with a possible worst-case scenario of IDR16,800 in the third quarter.
title: UOB Indonesia predicts 2025 inflation rate to rise to 2.5% due to higher food prices and weakening rupiah.

body: PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) recorded a net foreign buy of IDR 58.59 million, with a trading volume of 425.44 million shares worth IDR 50.07 billion, and its shares closed at IDR 118. PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk was the runner-up with a net foreign buy of IDR 88.58 million, with a trading volume of 171.81 million shares worth IDR 23.01 billion, and its shares closed at IDR 136.
title: Bukalapak tops net foreign buy list with 58.59 million shares purchased on Friday.

body: PT Trisula International Tbk (TRIS) is optimistic about achieving double-digit growth in 2025, driven by its strong sales and net profit performance in Q3 2024, with sales reaching IDR 1.08 trillion, up 4% year-over-year, and net profit rising 16% to IDR 62.23 billion. The company's strong operational cash flow, debt-to-equity ratio of 0.29x, and diversified business strategy, including exports and international subsidiaries, position it for continued growth despite industry challenges.
title: PT Trisula International TBK Aims for Double-Digit Growth in 2025 Despite Industry Challenges
